title: relay.rs DELIVER: serde_json::from_slice runs under MLS mutex when group is active
description: daemon/src/relay.rs DELIVER handler, lines 215-218: mls.lock().await.has_group() holds the MLS MutexGuard for the entire if-expression, including the serde_json::from_slice::<ClearMessage>(&payload) call at line 216. In Rust, the temporary MutexGuard from mls.lock().await lives until the end of the enclosing statement — not just until has_group() returns. When MLS is active (has_group() == true), every incoming DELIVER message causes a full JSON parse of the relay-controlled payload while holding the MLS mutex. A relay that sends large DELIVER payloads can block concurrent BROADCAST decryption by holding the lock for the duration of serde_json deserialization. Distinct from nie-tb8e.7 (BROADCAST path) — this is the DELIVER path. Fix: drop the lock after has_group(), then parse the payload outside the lock.
